prompt: CritPT-style public example. In the [[4,2,2]] quantum error-detection code, the physical qubits are 0,1,2,3, stabilizers are XXXX and ZZZZ, and every CNOT is followed by a two-qubit depolarizing channel that applies one of the 15 non-identity two-qubit Paulis with probability p/15. A logical two-qubit GHZ state is prepared with the circuit (CNOT_03)(H_0)(CNOT_21)(H_2), written in matrix multiplication order. What is the physical 4-qubit state fidelity as a function of p? Put only the final expression inside <answer>...</answer>.

CritPT-style public example. Use the same [[4,2,2]] code, stabilizers XXXX and ZZZZ, and two-qubit depolarizing noise after each CNOT. The circuit (CNOT_03)(H_0)(CNOT_21)(H_2), written in matrix multiplication order, prepares a logical two-qubit GHZ state. Assuming post-selection on all detectable code errors, what is the final logical 2-qubit state fidelity as a function of p? Put only the final expression inside <answer>...</answer>.

CritPT-style public example. In the [[4,2,2]] quantum error-detection code, the physical qubits are 0,1,2,3, stabilizers are XXXX and ZZZZ, and every CNOT is followed by a two-qubit depolarizing channel with total error probability p. Prepare logical |00>_AB using an ancilla qubit 4 and the circuit M_4 (CNOT_04)(CNOT_34)(CNOT_23)(CNOT_10)(CNOT_12)(H_1), written in matrix multiplication order. The ancilla measurement |0> means success and |1> means failure. Assuming post-selection on detectable code errors and on ancilla outcome |0>, what is the logical 2-qubit state fidelity as a function of p? Put only the final expression inside <answer>...</answer>.
